edges.evidence_quote: M23 is the principal member of the Congo River Alliance, a coalition of rebel groups in eastern DRC.
edges.context: M23 is the principal member of the Congo River Alliance.

edges.target: National Congress for the Defence of the People
edges.evidence_quote: M23 was established in 2012 by former members of the National Congress for the Defence of the People (CNDP)
edges.context: M23 was formed by former CNDP members.

edges.source: National Congress for the Defence of the People
edges.target: Armed Forces of the Democratic Republic of the Congo
edges.evidence_quote: These combatants had previously integrated into the Armed Forces of the Democratic Republic of the Congo (FARDC) under the terms of a 2009 peace agreement
edges.context: CNDP fighters integrated into FARDC under a 2009 peace agreement.

edges.target: Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda
edges.evidence_quote: The M23 is made up primarily of Tutsis and opposes the Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda (FDLR)
edges.context: M23 opposes the FDLR.

edges.source: National Congress for the Defence of the People
edges.target: Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda
edges.evidence_quote: From 2004 to 2009, CNDP activities were justified, in part, by the need to neutralize the FDLR.
edges.context: CNDP aimed to neutralize the FDLR.

edges.source: National Congress for the Defence of the People
edges.evidence_quote: Nkunda expanded his military and political base by incorporating General Bosco Ntaganda, the former chief of staff of the Union des Patriotes Congolais (UPC)
edges.context: CNDP incorporated the former chief of staff of UPC.

edges.source: National Congress for the Defence of the People
edges.evidence_quote: Ntaganda sought alliances with ex-combatants from the Patriotes Résistants Congolais (PARECO), a group historically opposed to the CNDP.
edges.context: CNDP sought alliances with PARECO ex-combatants.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Democratic Republic of the Congo, Wagner Group (allegedly), Wazalendo, Burundi, South Africa, Malawi, Tanzania, MONUSCO, FDLR
edges.context: M23 lists Wagner Group as an opponent.

edges.evidence_quote: Colonel Albert Kahasha, also called Foka Mike, deputy commander of the 808th FARDC Regiment, who joined the Mai-Mai PARECO group under La Fontaine in Bunyatenge, south of Lubero, in June 2012.
edges.context: A FARDC defector joined Mai-Mai PARECO, which was allied with M23.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2015, Bertrand Bisimwa emerged as one of the leaders of the new rebel group called M27, which is composed of members of the National Congress for the Defence of the People (CNDP) and M23 defectors.
edges.context: M27 is composed of M23 defectors, indicating a splinter relationship.

edges.source: Alliance des Patriotes pour un Congo Libre et Souverain
edges.target: Coalition des Mouvements pour le Changement/Forces de Défense du Peuple
edges.evidence_quote: On 9 May, various armed factions—including the Alliance des Patriotes pour un Congo Libre et Souverain (APCLS), the Coalition des Mouvements pour le Changement/Forces de Défense du Peuple (CMC/FDP), the Nduma Défense du Congo-Rénové (NDC-R), and the Alliance Nationale des Congolais pour la Défense des Droits de l'Homme (ANCDH)—signed an agreement in Pinga, Walikale Territory, pledging to halt hostilities among themselves and align with the Congolese army.
edges.context: These four factions signed an agreement to halt hostilities among themselves, indicating cooperation.

edges.source: Alliance des Patriotes pour un Congo Libre et Souverain

edges.context: M23 and FDLR are both proxy forces involved in conflict against FARDC, but the text does not specify direct cooperation or conflict between them.

edges.evidence_quote: M23 briefly occupied Kalembe in Walikale Territory before being repelled by Wazalendo
edges.context: Wazalendo fought against M23, repelling their attack.

edges.evidence_quote: M23 briefly occupied Kalembe in Walikale Territory before being repelled by Wazalendo and the Nduma Defense of Congo-Renovated
edges.context: Nduma Defense of Congo-Renovated fought alongside Wazalendo to repel M23.

edges.source: Auto-Défense des Communautés Victimes de l'Ituri
edges.target: Coopérative pour le Développement du Congo / Union des Révolutionnaires pour la Défense du Peuple Congolais
edges.evidence_quote: ADCVI engaged in provocative actions against rival groups, such as the Coopérative pour le Développement du Congo /Union des Révolutionnaires pour la Défense du Peuple Congolais (CODECO/URDPC)
edges.context: ADCVI is in conflict with CODECO/URDPC, engaging in provocative actions against them.

edges.source: Auto-Défense des Communautés Victimes de l'Ituri
edges.evidence_quote: ADCVI launched extensive recruitment drives across various 'G5' communities in the Djugu, Mahagi, and Aru territories, utilizing experienced former leaders and combatants from the Union des Patriotes Congolais (UPC)
edges.context: ADCVI recruited former UPC leaders and combatants, indicating a cooperative transfer of personnel.

edges.source: Auto-Défense des Communautés Victimes de l'Ituri
edges.evidence_quote: ADCVI reinforced its alliance with the AFC-M23 in preparation for planned offensive operations against FARDC in Ituri
edges.context: ADCVI is allied with AFC-M23, coordinating operations against FARDC.

edges.source: Force de Résistance Patriotique de l'Ituri
edges.evidence_quote: negotiations were reported in December 2024, indicating interest from the Force de Résistance Patriotique de l'Ituri (FRPI) and CODECO-URDPC in aligning with AFC-M23
edges.context: FRPI expressed interest in aligning with AFC-M23, indicating potential cooperation.

edges.source: Coopérative pour le Développement du Congo / Union des Révolutionnaires pour la Défense du Peuple Congolais
edges.evidence_quote: negotiations were reported in December 2024, indicating interest from the Force de Résistance Patriotique de l'Ituri (FRPI) and CODECO-URDPC in aligning with AFC-M23
edges.context: CODECO-URDPC expressed interest in aligning with AFC-M23, indicating potential cooperation.

edges.target: Auto-Défense des Communautés Victimes de l'Ituri
edges.evidence_quote: Though MAPI leaders opted for independence, they agreed to cooperate with ADCVI and AFC-M23
edges.context: MAPI agreed to cooperate with ADCVI despite remaining independent.
